title: brachyglottis bellidioides var. crassa
intro: Brachyglottis bellidioides var. crassa, also known as the Broad-leaved Tree Daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, Brachyglottis bellidioides var. crassa is often made into a tea or infused oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
